114.reason: [The input reading logic is flawed: it does not distinguish between the first and second polynomial inputs because both loops read until EOF. This causes the second loop to never execute since stdin is already exhausted after the first loop.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The input reading logic is flawed: it does not distinguish between the first and second polynomial inputs because both loops read until EOF. This causes the second loop to never execute since stdin is already exhausted after the first loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The program cannot distinguish between the end of the first polynomial and the start of the second because both loops read until EOF. The input consists of two lines, but scanf with %d skips whitespace including newlines, so the second loop never executes as the first consumes all input.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [The program cannot distinguish between the end of the first polynomial and the start of the second because it reads until EOF. The problem specifies that each polynomial is on a separate line ending with '\n', but this code reads all input as one continuous stream, causing both polynomials to be merged into poly1 or leading to incorrect parsing.]
